Package org.eventb.core.ast

Interface ITypeEnvironmentBuilder

All Superinterfaces:
ITypeEnvironment
All Known Subinterfaces:
IInferredTypeEnvironment
All Known Implementing Classes:
InferredTypeEnvironment, TypeEnvironmentBuilder
public interface ITypeEnvironmentBuilder extends ITypeEnvironment
Common protocol for building type environments.

See ITypeEnvironment for the general description.

When the type environment is built, an immutable copy of this environment could be retrieved by calling ITypeEnvironment.makeSnapshot() or a mutable copy by calling ITypeEnvironment.makeBuilder().

This interface is not intended to be implemented by clients. Use FormulaFactory.makeTypeEnvironment() to create new type environments.

Since:
3.0

  • Nested Class Summary

    Nested classes/interfaces inherited from interface org.eventb.core.ast.ITypeEnvironment

    ITypeEnvironment.IIterator

    Methods inherited from interface org.eventb.core.ast.ITypeEnvironment

  • Method Details

    • addAll

      void addAll(ITypeEnvironment other)
      Adds all mappings of the given type environment to this environment.

      All names that are common to this type environment and the given one must be associated with the same type in both type environments.

      Parameters:
      other - another type environment
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if the mappings of the given type environment are incompatible with this type environment
      IllegalArgumentException - if the given type environment formula factory is not the same as the current type environment formula factory

    • add

      void add(FreeIdentifier freeIdent)
      Adds the given free identifier to this environment.

      This is a convenience method, fully equivalent to addName(freeIdent.getName(), freeIdent.getType()).

      Parameters:
      freeIdent - a free identifier
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if the given free identifier is either not a valid identifier or not typed, or incompatible with this type environment
      IllegalArgumentException - if the free identifier formula factory is not the same as the type environment formula factory

    • addAll

      void addAll(FreeIdentifier[] freeIdents)
      Adds all given free identifiers to this environment.

      All given free identifiers must already be type-checked. All names that are common to this type environment and the given free identifiers must be associated with the same type.

      Parameters:
      freeIdents - array of free identifiers
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if any given free identifier is ill-formed
      IllegalArgumentException - if a free identifier formula factory is not the same as the type environment formula factory

    • addGivenSet

      void addGivenSet(String name)
      Adds a given set to this environment.

      The given name will be assigned its power set as type.

      If the given name already occurs in this environment, it must be associated with its power set.

      Parameters:
      name - the name to add

    • addName

      void addName(String name, Type type)
      Adds a name and its specified type in the type environment.

      If the given name already occurs in this environment, it must be associated with the given type. The given type is also analyzed to check that GivenType types used are defined (if not add them) or are coherent with the current environment.

      Parameters:
      name - the name to add
      type - the type to associate to the given name
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if the given name is not a valid identifier name or if the given type is not compatible with this type environment: different from a type already registered with the same name or containing incompatible carrier sets
      IllegalArgumentException - if the given type formula factory is not the same as the type environment formula factory

    • makeFreshIdentifiers

      FreeIdentifier[] makeFreshIdentifiers(BoundIdentDecl[] boundIdents)
      Creates fresh identifiers corresponding to some bound identifier declarations and inserts them into the type environment.

      For each bound identifier declaration, a free identifier is created. This new identifier has a name that does not occur in the given type environment and is based on the given declaration. It is guaranteed that not two free identifiers in the result bear the same name.

      The given bound identifier declarations must be typed. The types are then stored in the type environment for the corresponding fresh free identifiers.

      Parameters:
      boundIdents - array of bound identifier declarations for which corresponding fresh free identifiers should be created. Each declaration must be typed
      Returns:
      an array of fresh free identifiers
      Since:
      3.0

    • translate

      ITypeEnvironmentBuilder translate(FormulaFactory factory)
      Description copied from interface: ITypeEnvironment
      Returns a copy of this type environment built with the given formula factory.

      If the factory of this type environment and the given factory are the same, then this type environment is returned, rather than an identical copy of it. Otherwise a new type environment object is built.

      Calling this method when ITypeEnvironment.isTranslatable(FormulaFactory) returns false will raise an IllegalArgumentException.

      Specified by:
      translate in interface ITypeEnvironment
      Parameters:
      factory - the target formula factory
      Returns:
      an equivalent type environment built with the given factory
